title: Top 10 Modular Switches & Sockets in India (2026)
description: Top 10 modular switches & sockets in India for 2026 — Anchor Roma, Legrand Myrius, Havells Coral, Schneider ZENcelo & more, with 6A/16A ratings & buying advice.
author: Elume Editorial
intro: Modular switches are the small components that decide how a finished space actually feels to use — and how it ages. In Indian homes, offices and commercial fit-outs, the plate finish, the click feel of a 6A switch, the grip of a 3-pin 16A socket and the reliability of the modular frame all matter far more than the price tag suggests. For 2026 the market has matured: glass and metal finishes are mainstream, USB-charging sockets are standard SKUs, and ranges now span budget rewiring jobs to premium villa interiors.

When you shortlist a range, look at five things: contact rating (6A for lights and fans, 16A for geysers, ACs and heavy sockets), the number of modules a plate needs, switch mechanism life (silver-alloy contacts last longer), finish durability against fingerprints and yellowing, and the price tier versus the look you want. Match the range to the room, not just the brand name — that is where most procurement mistakes happen.

items.name: Roma & Roma Urban
items.body: Roma is the default modular range across Indian residential and rental projects, and for good reason — wide availability, dependable 6A and 16A switches, and a module ecosystem (1M, 2M, 3M and larger plates) that electricians know by heart. The classic Roma plates are matte white with a clean rocker, while Roma Urban steps up with glossy and metallic finishes for a more contemporary look. You get the full spread: 1-way and 2-way switches, bell pushes, 6A/16A combined sockets, fan regulators and USB charger modules. It is not the most luxurious range, but on cost-per-point, fitment ease and spares availability it is hard to beat for volume work.

items.name: Myrius & Myrius Nextgen
items.body: Myrius is Legrand's workhorse premium-modular range and a favourite for mid-to-upper residential and commercial fit-outs. Switches feel solid with a reassuring positive click, and the silver-alloy contacts are rated for long mechanism life — useful for high-traffic offices. The plate library is genuinely deep: glossy white, graphite, champagne and textured finishes, plus screwless plate options that hide fixings for a flush look. Module coverage is complete — 6A/16A switches, 1-way/2-way, 2-pin and 3-pin sockets, dimmers, fan regulators and USB-A/USB-C charger modules. Myrius Nextgen refreshes the aesthetics with slimmer profiles. A safe specification when you want a step above mass-market without jumping to glass-plate pricing.

items.name: ZENcelo
items.body: ZENcelo is Schneider's aspirational range and arguably the most design-led mainstream option in India. The flat, edge-to-edge switches with a soft tactile press feel distinctly premium, and the finishes — pearl white, dark grey, champagne gold and aluminium-effect — suit luxury apartments, boutique hotels and showrooms. It carries the expected 6A/16A switches, 2-way control, 3-pin 16A sockets and integrated USB charging, with indication LEDs on many modules. ZENcelo sits at a higher price tier and needs careful plate planning, but in spaces where the wall finish is part of the design language, it delivers a look that cheaper ranges simply cannot match. Pair it with Schneider's Livia for back-of-house economy.

items.name: Coral
items.body: Coral is Havells' broad, value-led modular range that has become a procurement staple for builders. It balances a clean, contemporary plate design with sensible pricing, and the module set covers everything a project needs: 6A and 16A switches, 1-way/2-way, bell pushes, fan regulators, 6A/16A multi-pin sockets and USB chargers. Finishes run from standard white to a few glossy and metallic options, so it photographs well in spec sheets without premium cost. Build quality is consistent and Havells' distribution means spares are rarely a problem. For developers fitting out multiple flats who want a recognisable brand at a controlled rate, Coral is one of the easiest decisions on this list.

items.name: Crabtree Athena & Murano
items.body: Crabtree, now under Havells, is the premium British-heritage brand for buyers who want a refined alternative to mass-market plates. Athena offers crisp, minimal switches with a satisfying mechanism, while Murano brings glass and mirror-finish plates for statement walls in living rooms and master suites. Ratings and configurations are complete — 6A/16A, 2-way, 3-pin 16A sockets, dimmers and USB modules — with strong attention to contact quality and finish durability. Pricing sits in the upper-mid to premium band. Choose Crabtree when the client cares about feel and finish and you want a name with genuine design credibility behind it, without going fully imported.

items.name: Levana
items.body: Polycab leveraged its enormous wiring and cable reach to push Levana hard, and it has become a credible mainstream modular range. The switches have a clean, slightly rounded aesthetic and a smooth press, with finishes spanning white, ivory and a few darker and metallic options. Module coverage is complete for Indian needs — 6A/16A switches, 1-way/2-way, 6A/16A sockets, fan regulators and USB charging modules. Because many projects already source Polycab wires and conduits, specifying Levana keeps the brand consistent and can simplify procurement. Pricing is competitive in the mid tier, making it a strong like-for-like alternative to the established names for contractors wanting to consolidate suppliers.

items.name: Livia & Opale
items.body: When ZENcelo is out of budget but you still want Schneider engineering, Livia and Opale fill the gap. Livia is the value range — neat white and glossy plates, reliable 6A/16A switches, 2-way control, 3-pin sockets and USB modules at a sensible price. Opale sits slightly above with more contemporary finishes and slimmer profiles for living areas. Both share Schneider's solid contact quality and safety features like shuttered sockets, and they mix well with ZENcelo so you can specify premium plates in feature rooms and economy plates in utility areas without changing brand. A smart way to control cost across a project while keeping a single, trusted manufacturer.

items.name: Mylinc
items.body: Mylinc is Legrand's economy modular range, aimed at projects that want the Legrand badge and contact reliability at a budget-friendly rate. The design is clean and unfussy — standard white plates with a dependable rocker — and the module set covers the essentials: 6A and 16A switches, 1-way/2-way, bell pushes, fan regulators, 6A/16A sockets and basic USB charging. You lose the premium finishes of Myrius, but you keep Legrand's mechanism quality and nationwide spares. For affordable housing, rental units, hostels and back-of-house areas where reliability matters more than aesthetics, Mylinc is a practical specification that still carries a name installers trust.

items.name: G-Series & Glamour
items.body: GM Modular is a strong value-to-premium player that often gets overlooked next to the big names. Its ranges span clean budget plates through to glass and metallic Glamour-style finishes that look far above their price. Module coverage is comprehensive — 6A/16A switches, 1-way/2-way, 16A 3-pin sockets, fan regulators, dimmers and USB charging modules — and many SKUs include features like indicator LEDs and child-safety shutters. Build quality has improved markedly and pricing is keen, which makes GM a smart choice for contractors who want a richer finish without paying top-tier rates. Worth shortlisting when you need premium looks on a tighter mid-market budget.

items.name: North-West Modular Range
items.body: Wipro rounds out the list as a dependable, no-drama modular option from a trusted Indian electrical brand. The plates are clean and contemporary, the switches have a firm, consistent action, and the range covers the standard requirements — 6A/16A switches, 1-way/2-way, 6A/16A sockets, fan regulators and USB charger modules. Finishes lean toward practical white and a few glossy options rather than statement glass. Where Wipro earns its place is reliability and value: solid contacts, sensible pricing and a brand procurement teams recognise. A sound pick for offices, institutional projects and homes where steady performance and easy availability outrank showpiece aesthetics.

buyingTips: Match the rating to the load: use 6A switches for lights, fans and small appliances, and 16A switches plus 3-pin 16A sockets for geysers, ACs, microwaves and water heaters. Mixing these up is the most common cause of nuisance tripping and overheating.

Plan modules before you buy plates. Count how many 1M slots each switch, socket and regulator needs, then size the plate (2M, 4M, 6M, 8M). Ordering plates and modules separately and in the right quantities avoids the classic mid-site shortage of a single blank or fuse module.

Prioritise contact quality for high-use points. Silver-alloy contacts and a positive, well-damped click indicate longer mechanism life — important for office switchboards and kitchens that get pressed thousands of times. For feature walls, glass and metal plates resist yellowing better than cheap glossy plastic.

Tier your specification across the project. Put premium ranges (ZENcelo, Crabtree Murano, Myrius) in living rooms and master suites, and economy ranges (Mylinc, Livia) in utility, staff and back-of-house areas — ideally within the same brand so finishes and plate sizes stay compatible.

faq.q: What is the difference between 6A and 16A modular switches?
faq.a: A 6A switch handles lighter loads — lights, ceiling fans and small appliances. A 16A switch and 16A 3-pin socket are built for heavy loads like geysers, air conditioners, microwaves and water heaters. Always match the switch and socket rating to the appliance; using a 6A point for a high-load device risks overheating and failure.

faq.q: What does 1-way and 2-way mean in a modular switch?
faq.a: A 1-way switch controls a light or fan from a single location — the standard for most points. A 2-way switch lets you control the same light from two places, such as the top and bottom of a staircase or both sides of a bedroom. Two 2-way switches are wired together to achieve this dual-control function.

faq.q: How many modules does a typical switch plate need?
faq.a: Modules are measured in 1M units. A single switch or socket is usually 1M, while a 16A socket or fan regulator can take 1M to 2M. Plates come in 1M, 2M, 3M, 4M, 6M and 8M sizes. Count the modules you need, add a blank for spares, then choose the matching plate size.

faq.q: Are USB-charging modular sockets worth specifying in 2026?
faq.a: Yes. USB sockets with USB-A and USB-C outputs are now standard SKUs across Anchor Roma Urban, Legrand Myrius, Havells Coral and Schneider ranges. They free up power sockets in bedrooms, living rooms and offices and add convenience clients notice. Specify them at bedsides, study desks and near seating for the best perceived value.

faq.q: Which modular brand offers the best value for builders in India?
faq.a: For volume residential work, Anchor Roma and Havells Coral lead on cost-per-point, availability and spares. For a single-vendor wiring-plus-switch solution, Polycab Levana is strong, while GM Modular delivers premium finishes at mid-market rates.
